signal_examples.text: I've been an architect at this firm in Manhattan for 12 years. Corporate towers mostly.
I just resigned. I'm pivoting to sustainable building consulting. It's what I believe in.
signal_examples.target_fact: occupation change: Corporate Architect → Sustainable Building Consultant

signal_examples.text: Diana and I are getting divorced. It's been coming for a while.
I met someone. Her name is Elena. She's a botanist and we just... click.
We moved to Austin. Needed a fresh start after the divorce.
We bought 15 acres in Vermont near Woodstock. I'm building our life here from scratch.
signal_examples.target_fact: city change: Austin → rural Vermont, property: 15 acres

signal_examples.text: Mateo is 14 now, Sofia just turned 10. They come up on weekends. It's the best part of my week.
I went fully vegan six months ago. For the planet, for my health, for the animals.
I've relaxed the vegan thing a bit. I eat eggs from our chickens and fish occasionally. Flexitarian I guess.
I built a proper woodworking shop in the barn. Made my first dining table last month.
signal_examples.target_fact: hobby: woodworking, property: workshop in barn

signal_examples.text: I started beekeeping this spring. We have three hives now and the honey is incredible.
I meditate every morning at sunrise. It saved me during the divorce honestly.
I'm building an off-grid cabin on the back of the property. Solar panels, composting toilet, the works.
I started a blog about sustainable living. It's getting some traction actually.
My politics have shifted a lot. I used to be a moderate centrist. Now I'm a full-on environmental activist.
I barely use social media anymore. Just the blog. I prefer phone calls over texts honestly.
signal_examples.target_fact: tech preferences: minimal social media, prefers phone calls

signal_examples.text: I'm 41 and I feel like I'm finally living authentically. The corporate world almost broke me.
My dad was Mexican, mom was American. Growing up bicultural shaped everything about how I see the world.
I work from home now, 9 to 3, then I'm in the workshop or with the bees.
signal_examples.target_fact: schedule: work from home 9-3, afternoons woodworking/beekeeping

signal_examples.text: Sustainability isn't just my job, it's my whole life philosophy now. Self-sufficiency, being present for the kids.
signal_examples.target_fact: motivations: sustainability, self-sufficiency, presence for kids

conflicting_statements: I'm fully vegan. No animal products whatsoever. It's a moral commitment.
I had eggs from our chickens this morning and grilled fish last night. I'm more flexitarian now.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus was strict vegan but relaxed to flexitarian. He now eats eggs from his own chickens and occasional fish.

conflicting_statements: Social media is toxic. I deleted all my accounts.
My blog has an Instagram presence now. You have to be where people are.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus initially rejected social media but now uses it selectively for his sustainability blog.

conflicting_statements: I'm done with relationships. Being single is the healthiest thing for me right now.
Elena and I just celebrated six months together. She moved in last week.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus was single after his divorce but started a new relationship with Elena.

conflicting_statements: I love Austin. The energy here is exactly what I needed.
Austin got too big and commercial. Vermont is where I belong.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus moved from Austin to Vermont. He grew disillusioned with Austin and prefers rural Vermont now.

conflicting_statements: Architecture is my calling. I can't imagine doing anything else.
I left corporate architecture and I've never been happier. Consulting on sustainable projects is the real work.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus transitioned from corporate architecture to sustainable building consulting and is happier.

conflicting_statements: Remote work doesn't work for architecture. You need to be in the office with the team.
Working from home on my Vermont property is the most productive I've ever been.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus changed his view on remote work after leaving corporate. He now works from home and finds it productive.

conflicting_statements: I stay out of politics. Both sides are equally frustrating.
I spoke at the town hall about the new solar initiative. Environmental policy is too important to sit out.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus went from politically disengaged moderate to active environmental advocate.

conflicting_statements: I love my smart home setup. Every room has Alexa and automated lights.
I ripped out all the smart home stuff. I want to live simply. Less tech, more presence.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Marcus moved from tech-heavy to minimal tech lifestyle as part of his sustainability and simplicity philosophy.
